title: Grazielia dimorpholepis
taxonomy: Grazielia dimorpholepis is a species of flowering plant in the family Acanthaceae. It is native to Brazil and belongs to the genus Grazielia. The classification of Grazielia dimorpholepis places it within the kingdom Plantae, phylum Angiospermophyta, class Magnoliopsida, and order Lamiales. The plant's classification is further broken down into family Acanthaceae, genus Grazielia, and species G. dimorpholepis. This detailed classification helps in identifying and differentiating Grazielia dimorpholepis from other similar species.

The plant is rich in various bioactive constituents, including flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which have been attributed to its numerous health benefits, including antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Grazielia dimorpholepis include its potential to protect against oxidative stress, inflammation, and microbial infections, as well as its ability to modulate immune responses and exhibit anticancer properties.
